description: HL7 v2.4 ORU^R01 CBC result from an EDAN H60S 3-part hematology analyzer (MLLP; the analyzer is the TCP client, our edge listens). Wire format graduated to the real EDANLAB profile confirmed at the 2026-07-06 physical H60S bench (LIS-20): sending app MSH-3 'H60^7907', facility MSH-4 'EDANLAB', analyte code in OBX-4 (OBX-3 is a 0/1 suspect flag), patient number in PID-2, sample id in OBR-2, HL7-escaped units, and a trailing OBX-12 value^unit. CBC values are representative (the bench had blank material only, so real analyte values were 0/---); the byte-exact real capture is archived with the bench evidence.

source.reference: Wire format from 2026-07-06 physical EDAN H60S bench (LIS-20; SN 860575-M26409800004, firmware wire-id H60^7907; run 20260703T134217Z-h60s). Byte-exact real capture: bench-runs/20260703T134217Z-h60s/h60s-oru-numeric.hl7.
source.note: Wire format validated at the 2026-07-06 bench: the H60S emits the EDAN H90-family layout, NOT the clean-HL7 layout this seed originally assumed. MSH-4='EDANLAB' (not 'EDAN'), MSH-3='H60^7907' (not 'H60S'), analyte code in OBX-4 with OBX-3 a 0/1 suspect flag, patient number in PID-2, sample id in OBR-2. The edge parser routes on MSH-4=='EDANLAB' (_is_edan_h90) to read OBX-4/PID-2/OBR-2. Units carry the HL7 caret escape 10\S\9/L (== 10^9/L) and there is a trailing OBX-12 value^unit component the parser ignores. The real bench message was blank material (WBC/RBC/HGB/PLT numeric, HCT/MCV '---'); this fixture keeps the six-CBC profile with representative values so it remains the Stage-1 normalize/milestone vehicle. Real markers seen but out of scope here: '***' no-result sentinel, MSH-16=2 on the connection-test ping, ~33 analytes on a full frame.
